office_does: One of two House members for LD 48 (Redmond, Bellevue, Kirkland area); serves a two-year term voting on state budgets, taxes, and all state law.
race_blurb: A Democrat-vs-Democrat contest: incumbent Rep. Amy Walen, a self-described pragmatic Democrat in the moderate/business lane, is challenged from the progressive/labor lane by Redmond City Councilmember Jessica Forsythe. Both advance to the general.

candidates.summary: Incumbent state representative since 2019, chair of the House Consumer Protection & Business Committee, and former Kirkland mayor who self-describes as a 'pragmatic governing Democrat.'
highlights: Says she voted no on 'the income tax' and champions balanced budgets and fiscal responsibility [S1]
Backs a 'balanced approach' to public safety: community-focused law enforcement plus expanded mental health and crisis response; endorsed by the Bellevue Police Guild [S1][S2]
Cites votes banning ICE from wearing masks and backfilling Planned Parenthood funding [S1]

experience.basis: House member since 2019 with a committee chairmanship, plus a decade in Kirkland city government including the mayoralty.

taxes.basis: Highlights her no vote on 'the income tax' (her framing of the capital gains tax) and tax relief for families and small businesses.

spending.basis: Emphasizes balanced budgets, fiscal responsibility, and combating wasteful spending, citing the regional homelessness authority.

housing.basis: Supports both housing investment and missing-middle supply reform; no tenant-protection emphasis — mixed on this axis.

safety.basis: Explicitly 'balanced approach': investment in community-focused law enforcement (Bellevue Police Guild endorsement) alongside expanded mental health and crisis response.

climate.basis: Supports clean energy and low-carbon fuel standards.

immigration.basis: Cites her vote to ban ICE from wearing masks.

social.basis: Prioritizes protecting reproductive healthcare access; cites backfilling Planned Parenthood funding.

candidates.summary: Redmond city councilmember since 2020 and past council president challenging the incumbent from the progressive/labor lane, with the local Democratic-organization and state labor council endorsements.
highlights: Cites winning tenant protections and securing $7.5M for housing and human services on the Redmond council [S1][S2]
Co-authored Redmond's Climate Emergency Declaration; chaired the council's Alternative Crisis Response Subcommittee [S1][S2]
Endorsed by the 48th District Democrats, King County Democrats, WA State Labor Council, and Sen. Vandana Slatter [S1]

experience.basis: Challenger with a municipal record (Redmond council since 2020) but no state legislative record, running against a 7-year incumbent.

housing.basis: Cites winning tenant protections and securing $7.5M for housing and human services; campaigns on renter protections.

climate.basis: Co-authored Redmond's Climate Emergency Declaration and its Environmental Sustainability Action Plan; campaigns on climate resiliency.

safety.basis: Chaired Redmond's Alternative Crisis Response Subcommittee.

spending.basis: Campaigns on equitable K-12 funding and public investment in housing, human services, and behavioral health.

social.basis: Campaigns on reproductive rights; leads Emerge WA training women and non-binary leaders; Planned Parenthood endorsement.
